skill.name: hig-project-context
skill.description: Create or update a shared Apple design context document that other HIG skills use to tailor guidance. Use when the user says 'set up my project context,' 'what platforms am I targeting,' 'configure HIG settings,' or when starting a new Apple platform project.
skill.summary: Create or update a shared Apple design context document that other HIG skills use to tailor guidance...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and hardcoded URL patterns. Review found the shell and cryptography alerts are Markdown false positives, while the URL is a publisher link and the skill has expected workspace file access.

low_findings.title: Workspace Filesystem Context Access
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read project metadata and create or update `.claude/apple-design-context.md`. This is expected for the skill, but users should understand it operates on workspace project files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly name files to read and a context document to generate or update. The behavior is legitimate for this skill and limited to project context handling.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Publisher Links
low_findings.description: The static network findings are outbound Markdown links to the publisher website. No automatic request, tracking code, credential transfer, or data exfiltration pattern was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 128 contains only Markdown hyperlinks. There is no executable network call or instruction to send project data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown code spans and a fenced document template. They do not execute commands or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown syntax in a documentation file. There is no script file, interpreter invocation, or command execution context.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings do not correspond to cryptographic APIs, hashing, encryption, or authentication logic. The cited content is product guidance text and Markdown formatting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each cited area found no crypto implementation. The matches appear to come from generic text tokens in Markdown.

content.value_statement: Apple platform design guidance often needs repeated project details before it can be useful. This skill creates a shared context document so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can tailor HIG guidance faster.
actual_capabilities: Creates a `.claude/apple-design-context.md` document for Apple design work.
Checks existing project context before asking follow-up questions.
Guides discovery of platforms, minimum OS versions, and Apple technologies.
Captures design system, accessibility, user persona, and asset information.
Supports updates that preserve unchanged context sections.
limitations: It does not validate designs against the full Apple HIG by itself.
It depends on accurate user confirmation of discovered project details.
It is focused on Apple platform projects, not web or Android products.
It may need other HIG skills for component-level recommendations.

use_cases.title: Start an Apple App Design Review
use_cases.description: Create a reusable context file before asking for HIG guidance on a new app.

use_cases.title: Align Team Design Decisions
use_cases.description: Document platforms, accessibility targets, and design system choices for shared AI assistance.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Redesign Brief
use_cases.description: Capture current app state, known challenges, and user context before planning redesign changes.

prompt_templates.title: Create Project Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new Apple platform project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up my Apple HIG project context for this app. Inspect the repository, summarize what you find, and ask only for missing details.

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Refresh the shared context after project changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update my Apple design context. Read the existing context file, ask what changed, and preserve all unchanged sections.

prompt_templates.title: Document Accessibility Needs
prompt_templates.scenario: Add accessibility expectations before design review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Apple design context and help me complete the accessibility section for VoiceOver, Dynamic Type, and regulatory requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Multi-Platform Guidance
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced setup for apps targeting several Apple platforms.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete Apple HIG project context for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and visionOS. Include platform differences, technology choices, and design constraints.

output_examples.input: Set up my project context for a SwiftUI iPad app.
output_examples.output: A concise Apple design context with product summary, iPadOS target, SwiftUI stack, accessibility goals, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Update the context because we added watchOS support.
output_examples.output: An updated context summary that changes the platform table and notes watchOS-specific use cases while preserving other sections.

output_examples.input: Help me prepare for HIG component recommendations.
output_examples.output: A completed context brief covering platforms, design system status, user environments, and known component challenges.

best_practices: Confirm auto-discovered project facts before saving the shared context file.
Keep the context concise so later HIG skills can read it quickly.
Update the context whenever platform targets, accessibility goals, or design assets change.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill as a substitute for detailed HIG compliance review.
Do not store secrets, credentials, or private customer data in the context file.
Do not overwrite existing context without reviewing what changed.

faq.question: What file does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or updates `.claude/apple-design-context.md` in the project workspace.

faq.question: Does it work with SwiftUI and UIKit projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks which UI framework is used and supports mixed Apple app stacks.

faq.question: Can it handle multiple Apple platforms?
faq.answer: Yes. The context template includes iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, and visionOS.

faq.question: Does it make final design decisions?
faq.answer: No. It records project context so other HIG guidance can be more specific.

faq.question: Should I include confidential business data?
faq.answer: No. Include only design and product context needed for Apple platform guidance.

faq.question: Why does it inspect repository files?
faq.answer: It uses existing files to infer platforms, frameworks, assets, and accessibility coverage before asking questions.
